prevalence.source: Adolescent health surveys and gynecology epidemiology syntheses

incidence.value: Common at menarche and adolescence; recurrent monthly episodes

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate QOL and school/work absenteeism impact

vulnerablePopulations: women with limited access to gynecology care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis for primary dysmenorrhea; investigate secondary causes when severe or refractory

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of millions of women affected
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with NSAIDs and hormonal therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and gynecology assessment; ultrasound for secondary causes when indicated
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: NSAIDs, combined oral contraceptives/progestins, lifestyle measures; referral for suspected endometriosis
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma and delayed evaluation for secondary causes
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable NSAIDs and hormonal therapies widely available
